I see - this approach is wrong because it breaks the entire function. Let me view the actual NewRepository function first to understand its signature:
Now I understand. Instead of adding to body, I need to use the param op properly. Let me try with an explicit `nil` as default:

I understand - `ago_add_param` requires a `default` value because it updates ALL call sites automatically. But in this case, we're adding context everywhere, which means existing callers don't have a context to pass.

The right approach here is to:
1. Add the param with a meaningful default (like `nil`, since the caller will need to provide their own context anyway)
2. Then manually update each caller to pass the appropriate context

part.text:
---

## Goal
- Add `ctx context.Context` as second parameter to all `NewRepository` functions across the repository for context-carrying error handling

## Constraints & Preferences
- Must be applied across whole repository (12+ packages)
- Project must typecheck after changes
- Use `ago_add_param` where possible with default value

## Progress
### Done
- [done] github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/auth/password - added ctx via ago_patch, 62 call sites updated
- [done] github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/iam - added ctx via ago_patch, 36 call sites updated
- [done] github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/authtoken - added ctx via ago_patch, 34 call sites updated

### In Progress
- [blocked] github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/auth/oidc - attempting to add ctx fails with "ctx redeclared in this block" error
